purpose: Choose target normal form depth and when to denormalize for usability, performance, or analytics.
required_context: who writes queries and maintains the schema
ask_if_missing: Which anomalies or duplicate facts are causing real risk?
What join/query patterns must remain simple and fast?
assume_if_low_risk: target 3NF for OLTP unless deeper normalization has a concrete integrity payoff
prefer star-like denormalized structures for analytical warehouse access

branches.criteria: OLTP with concurrent updates and clear dependencies
branches.recommendation: normalize through 3NF, enforce primary/foreign keys, and consider deeper forms only for concrete anomaly risk

branches.criteria: beyond-3NF split mainly creates tiny tables or many joins
branches.recommendation: stop before over-normalization; enforce edge rules in constraints/application logic if simpler

branches.criteria: warehouse or reporting workload over large history
branches.recommendation: denormalize into facts and dimensions; avoid deep normalized joins in analyst-facing structures

branches.criteria: denormalization duplicates values or stores derived values
branches.recommendation: define source of truth, refresh/update path, reconciliation, and stale-value tolerance

failure_modes: normalizing because a higher normal form exists, not because it helps
leaving repeating groups/list fields in OLTP tables
creating many-to-many resolution tables with no business meaning or application need
denormalizing without ownership of duplicate/derived facts
mistaking better integrity for better query performance in all workloads
output_checks: states rejected deeper normal forms and why
